title: Array
subtitle: Store multiple values
goal: Learn to use arrays to store multiple values in a single variable.
explanation: An Array is an ordered list of values.
An array can hold many elements.
Elements are written inside square brackets [].
Each element has its own number called an index.
Arrays are used in almost every program.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine a box containing several fruits. Instead of using a separate box for each fruit, you store them all in one container.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners often think the first index is 1. In fact, array indexes start at 0.
